zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah de Beauchamp於大約1250年出生在英國肯特艾爾姆利時，她的父親William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick14歲，母親Maud FitzJohn15歲。她於1269年1月7日與Richard de Talbot在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1317年7月在她的家鄉享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah de Beauchamp于大约1250年出生在英国肯特艾爾姆利时，她的父亲William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick14岁，母亲Maud FitzJohn15岁。她于1269年1月7日与Richard de Talbot在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1317年7月在她的家乡享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah de Beauchamp was born about 1250, in Elmley, Kent, England, her father, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, was 14 and her mother, Maud FitzJohn, was 15. She married Richard de Talbot on 7 January 1269, in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in July 1317, in her hometown, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah de Beauchamp est née environ 1250, à Elmley, Kent, Angleterre, son père, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Maud FitzJohn, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Richard de Talbot le 7 janvier 1269, à Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en juillet 1317, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sarah de Beauchamp um 1250 in Elmley, Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick 14 und ihre Mutter Maud FitzJohn 15. Sie hat am 7. Januar 1269 in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom Richard de Talbot geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb im Juli 1317 im Alter von 68 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah de Beauchamp è nata il circa 1250 nel Elmley, Kent, England, sua padre, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, aveva 14 anni e sua madre, Maud FitzJohn, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Richard de Talbot il 7 gennaio 1269, nel Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel luglio 1317, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sarah de Beauchampが約1250年にイギリスケントエルムリーで生まれた時，父William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwickは14歳，母Maud FitzJohnは15歳であった。1269年1月7日にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエルムリー カストルでRichard de Talbotと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1317年7月に故郷で68歳で死去した。

ko.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: 영국 웨스트모를랜드 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1250년 영국 켄트 엘리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick(은)는 14세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud FitzJohn(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1269년 1월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 엘리 카스틀에서 Richard de Talbot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1317년 7월 그녀의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah de Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1250, em Elmley, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, tinha 14 anos e sua mãe, Maud FitzJohn, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard de Talbot em 7 de janeiro de 1269, em Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em julho de 1317, em sua cidade natal, com 68 anos.

ru.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, вестморланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah de Beauchamp родилась около 1250 г. в местности элмли, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud FitzJohn, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de Talbot 7 января 1269 г. в местности элмли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла июль 1317 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 68 лет.

es.Elmley Castle, Worcester, England: Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sarah de Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1250, en Elmley, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, tenía 14 años y su mamá, Maud FitzJohn, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Richard de Talbot el 7 de enero de 1269, en Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en julio de 1317, en su pueblo, a la edad de 68 años.
